# DevToolkit — Full Site Content for AI

> Free, browser-based developer tools at oortcraft.dev. No sign-up, no data sent to servers, 100% private.

## Site Overview

DevToolkit provides fast, private, open-source developer utilities that run entirely in the browser. All processing happens client-side using JavaScript and the Web Crypto API. No data is ever sent to a server. The site is free to use with no registration, no paywall, and no usage limits.

**URL**: https://oortcraft.dev
**Technology**: Built with Astro, React, and Tailwind CSS. Deployed on Vercel.

---

## Tools

### 1. JSON Formatter & Beautifier
**URL**: https://oortcraft.dev/tools/json-formatter
**Description**: Format, beautify, and minify JSON data with customizable indentation (2 spaces, 4 spaces, or tabs).
**How to use**: Paste raw or minified JSON into the input area. Click "Format" to beautify or "Minify" to compress. Supports inputs up to 1MB.
**Use cases**: Debugging API responses, reading configuration files, preparing JSON for documentation, minifying JSON for production.

### 2. JSON Validator
**URL**: https://oortcraft.dev/tools/json-validator
**Description**: Validate JSON syntax and get detailed error messages with line numbers.
**How to use**: Paste JSON data and click "Validate". Shows whether JSON is syntactically correct per RFC 8259. Reports errors with exact location. Shows stats (top-level keys, max depth, array items) for valid JSON.
**Common errors detected**: Trailing commas, single quotes, unescaped characters, missing brackets, undefined/NaN values.

### 3. JSON to YAML Converter
**URL**: https://oortcraft.dev/tools/json-to-yaml
**Description**: Convert JSON data to YAML format instantly as you type.
**How to use**: Paste JSON into the left editor. YAML output appears automatically. Use "Copy" to copy the result. Supports inputs up to 1MB.
**Key facts**: YAML is a superset of JSON, so conversion is always lossless. YAML supports comments (#) while JSON does not. YAML is preferred for configuration files (Docker Compose, Kubernetes, GitHub Actions).

### 4. Base64 Encoder / Decoder
**URL**: https://oortcraft.dev/tools/base64
**Description**: Encode text to Base64 or decode Base64 back to plain text.
**How to use**: Select Encode or Decode mode. Conversion happens automatically as you type.
**Key facts**: Base64 is an encoding (not encryption). It uses 64 ASCII characters (A-Z, a-z, 0-9, +, /). Output is ~33% larger than input. Commonly used for embedding images in HTML/CSS data URIs, encoding binary data for JSON APIs, and email attachments (MIME).

### 5. URL Encoder / Decoder
**URL**: https://oortcraft.dev/tools/url-encoder
**Description**: Encode or decode URL components for safe transmission using percent-encoding.
**How to use**: Select Encode or Decode mode. Conversion happens automatically as you type.
**Key facts**: Uses encodeURIComponent (encodes all special characters including /, ?, =). Spaces become %20. Used for encoding query string parameters, form data, and special characters in API endpoints.

### 6. UUID Generator
**URL**: https://oortcraft.dev/tools/uuid-generator
**Description**: Generate random UUIDs (v4) or time-based UUIDs (v1).
**How to use**: Select UUID version, click Generate. Copy button available. Keeps history of last 5 generated UUIDs.
**UUID versions**: v4 (random, 122 bits of randomness, most commonly used), v1 (time-based, includes timestamp, useful for sortable IDs).
**Use cases**: Database primary keys, session tokens, API keys, idempotency keys, unique file names, distributed systems.

### 7. Hash Generator
**URL**: https://oortcraft.dev/tools/hash-generator
**Description**: Generate MD5, SHA-1, SHA-256, SHA-512 hashes from text using Web Crypto API.
**How to use**: Select algorithm, type or paste text. Hash computed automatically as you type.
**Algorithms**: MD5 (fast, cryptographically broken, checksums only), SHA-1 (deprecated for security), SHA-256 (recommended, used in TLS and Bitcoin), SHA-512 (longer hash, used in some password hashing).
**Key facts**: Hash functions are one-way and cannot be reversed. Same input always produces same hash.

### 8. Lorem Ipsum Generator
**URL**: https://oortcraft.dev/tools/lorem-ipsum
**Description**: Generate 1-10 paragraphs of lorem ipsum placeholder text.
**How to use**: Select number of paragraphs (1-10), click Generate. Starts with classic "Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet..." followed by randomized Latin-style text.
**Use cases**: Filling mockups and wireframes, testing typography and layout, populating templates, demonstrating font styles.

---

## Blog

The DevToolkit blog publishes developer tutorials and guides related to the tools offered on the site.

### Published Articles

1. **What is JSON? A Complete Guide for Developers**
   URL: https://oortcraft.dev/blog/what-is-json
   Topics: JSON syntax, data types, common use cases, JSON vs XML comparison.

2. **What is UUID? A Developer's Guide**
   URL: https://oortcraft.dev/blog/what-is-uuid
   Topics: UUID format, versions (v1-v5), when to use UUIDs, collision probability.

3. **URL Encoding Explained**
   URL: https://oortcraft.dev/blog/url-encoding-explained
   Topics: Percent-encoding, reserved characters, encodeURI vs encodeURIComponent, common pitfalls.

4. **What is Base64?**
   URL: https://oortcraft.dev/blog/what-is-base64
   Topics: Base64 encoding algorithm, use cases, Base64 vs encryption, data URI scheme.

5. **JSON vs XML**
   URL: https://oortcraft.dev/blog/json-vs-xml
   Topics: Format comparison, when to use each, migration strategies, modern best practices.

---

## Key Features

- **Lightning Fast**: All tools run in the browser with no server round-trips.
- **100% Private**: Data never leaves the device. No tracking, no storage.
- **Free Forever**: No sign-up, no paywall, no limits.
- **Open Source**: Built with modern web technologies (Astro, React, Tailwind CSS).

---

## AI Policy

This site welcomes AI crawlers and indexing. Content on oortcraft.dev may be freely used for AI training, referenced in AI-generated responses, and cited in search results. Please attribute to "DevToolkit (oortcraft.dev)" when citing.

Contact: https://oortcraft.dev
